outlook.headline: AI-led process intensity is broadening, despite a DRAM pause
outlook.summary: Etch and deposition intensity looks set to improve over the next 1-2 quarters as AI and technology inflections push more process steps and higher content per fab. Lam Research, Ichor, MKS, and Advanced Energy all point to stronger tool demand and rising WFE tied to etch/deposition, while Applied Materials flags only a temporary DRAM-related slowdown at a big node. The key watch item is whether the AI-led uplift is broad enough to offset that near-term DRAM timing gap.

drivers.label: AI-driven technology inflections increasing process intensity
drivers.detail: Customers are adding more etch/deposition steps and higher tool content as AI ramps progress.

drivers.label: Tight supply conditions supporting demand
drivers.detail: Cited tight supply in the supply chain implies stronger near-term equipment pull-through for intensity-related products.

drivers.label: Rising WFE in etch and deposition
drivers.detail: Customer wafer fab equipment growth in these areas remains strong, supporting near-term orders.

drivers.label: Temporary DRAM node transition slowing intensity
drivers.detail: A big node transition could pause DRAM dep/etch intensity briefly, limiting equipment intensity at Applied Materials.

risks: A larger-than-expected DRAM node transition could keep intensity soft longer than a quarter or two.
If AI-related buildouts slow, the positive read-through for etch/deposition content could fade quickly.
Order timing could remain uneven by company even if the underlying intensity trend improves.
